Description: An artificial construct animated by ancient mechanisms. You were built to serve a function that no longer exists or a master who is gone. You seek purpose.
Starting Talents: Natural AR 1 (constructed body - no armor slot needed). Immune to poison and disease conditions. Repair with tools rather than healing (1 treasure of parts per slot instead of healer).
Starting Equipment: Relic power cell (x2). Tool kit. Heavy weapon of your creator's design. No rations needed.
Notes: Exceptional background. Most settlements have never seen a Golem. Reactions will vary wildly. Cannot benefit from HEAL - use MEND or IRON instead.

Description: You were born sensitive to spirits - able to hear them speak and negotiate with them. Your community relied on you as their link to the spiritual world.
Starting Talents: Spirit Sense (detect presence of spirits at will - 2-in-6 to commune briefly without casting). RING rune known at start.
Starting Equipment: Ritual focus (instrument/fetish/staff d6). Travelling clothes. Spirit vessel (one spirit already bound). 1 week rations.
Notes: CHA governs MP. Spell list emphasizes nature elemental and spirit magic. Learns new runes by communing with spirits rather than research.

Description: You spent years learning the old systems - the locks security mechanisms and inscriptions of the ancient world. Someone paid you to get in and out of places.
Starting Talents: Old-world Interface (2-in-6 to operate ancient mechanisms read rune inscriptions identify relic tech - treated as background warrant). Lockpicks (no slot).
Starting Equipment: Lockpicks. Crowbar. Dagger d4. Light armor AR 1. Relic data tablet. 2 weeks rations.
Notes: Hunter: relies on skill and improvisation in combat. Caster: uses rune knowledge to interface with old-world magic directly. Either way the Delver is the party member who handles the puzzle rooms.

Description: You survived by taking what you needed. Theft extortion smuggling - the specific trade varies but the skills translate.
Starting Talents: Thief (d6 rolls for lockpicking stealth sleight of hand climbing. Stealth attacks: max damage + roll weapon die again vs AR normally).
Starting Equipment: Dagger d4 (x2). Dark clothes (counts as light armor AR 1 for stealth purposes only). Lockpicks. 50ft rope. 1 week rations. Small amount of coin (<1 treasure).
Notes: No heavy armor. The criminal background does not specify lawful or chaotic - some thieves have codes others do not.

Description: You know the wilderness - how to move through it survive in it and read what it tells you. Whether you were a scout a hunter or simply someone who preferred trees to walls.
Starting Talents: Ranger (d6 rolls for wilderness survival tracking stealth in outdoor environments. Critical hit range expands to 19-20 against beasts).
Starting Equipment: Bow d6 + quiver. Short sword d4. Light armor AR 1. Hunting kit. 2 weeks rations.
Notes: Hunter: the wilderness combat specialist. Caster: nature magic and spirit communion in the wild places. No heavy armor.

Description: You sold your skills to whoever paid. You have seen enough to know that most causes are equally valid and equally temporary.
Starting Talents: Blade Dancer (melee ATK and DEF +1 with medium weapon) OR Archer/Gunner (ATK and damage +1 with chosen ranged weapon).
Starting Equipment: Sword d6. Medium armor AR 2. Shield. 1 week rations. Small purse (<1 treasure).
Notes: Hunter only. The default fighting person. Straightforward to play.

Description: You serve an institution - a church a cult a recovered-tech order like the Ethos. Your faith may be genuine political or somewhere between.
Starting Talents: Blessed (heal 1d6 + WIS/CHA once per day on touch. d6 rolls for religious lore ceremony faith-based persuasion).
Starting Equipment: Holy symbol or relic focus. Light armor AR 1. Mace d6 OR pistol d6 (relic - if Ethos-type). Scroll (CURE or HEAL). 1 week rations.
Notes: Hunter: armed minister martial order inquisitor. Caster: miracle-worker channeling divine force. The Ethos church priest is a Hunter with relic tech. The spirit-healer is a Caster.

Description: You performed - music dance acrobatics or something stranger. You learned to read crowds manage dangerous people and occasionally vanish when necessary.
Starting Talents: Jester (d6 rolls for performance deception reading a crowd. Reroll any single die that affects you once per day).
Starting Equipment: Instrument (no slots). Dagger d4. Travelling clothes. Disguise kit. 1 week rations. Small purse (<1 treasure).
Notes: Hunter: bard who handles problems with steel wit and presence. Caster: one who weaves magic into performance - illusion mind and sound runes feel natural here.

Description: You were raised in a noble household - a great family a merchant dynasty or a powerful guild. You learned politics resource management and how power actually works.
Starting Talents: Houseborn (d6 rolls for etiquette negotiation political lore and identifying social hierarchies. +1 to reaction rolls in civilized settings).
Starting Equipment: Fine clothes (counts as light armor AR 1 in social contexts - people hesitate to strike). Sword d6 OR spellbook (blank). Letter of introduction (opens doors once). 1 week rations.
Notes: Hunter: noble scion trained in arms. Caster: educated in arcane arts as a mark of status. The letter of introduction is a one-use social resource - the referee determines what doors it opens.

Description: You studied the runic language of the ancients - academically rigorously. You know the theory even where the practice is dangerous.
Starting Talents: Academic (d6 rolls for arcane lore history languages and identifying magical effects. WIS score caps spells known as normal but you begin knowing one additional rune).
Starting Equipment: Spellbook. Staff d6. Travelling robes. Ink and quill. 2 week rations. One Tier 1 rune known of choice + one additional rune of referee's choice from an obscure source.
Notes: Caster only. WIS governs MP. Learns new runes through research libraries and old-world inscriptions.

Description: You were cast out. Excommunicated from a house, exiled from a community, stripped of rank, or simply fled something that wanted you dead. The reasons are your own. You carry whatever you could take when you left.
Starting Talents: Outcast's Eye (2-in-6 to read the social dynamics of any group - who holds power who is afraid who is lying. Hard-won from surviving on the outside). One additional starting talent from your background reflecting what you were before.
Starting Equipment: Travelling clothes (worn). One quality item from your former life (weapon tool or symbol of status - worth 1-2 treasure). Dagger d4. 1 week rations. A contact from your former life who owes you or fears you.
Notes: Genos-agnostic. A Kith noble exile. A Ctarl shaman driven out by their spirits. A Kreen Pariah. A Goblin who left the colony. The Outcast background works for any genos and any type. The one additional talent represents who you were before - take it from the talent list with referee approval.
